_comment: Deployments-API payload for the daily AI brief's Managed Agents scheduled deployment.
Source-of-truth per docs/adr/0006 FR-16/AC-15 — this file is what defines the
schedule and which self-hosted environment/agent it targets. It is NOT applied
automatically by this CDK stack; create/update the deployment via the Deployments
API or Claude Console using this file's values (see deploy/managed-agent/README.md).
Built against beta header: managed-agents-2026-04-01 (PRD FR-2). If Anthropic
changes this API surface, this file's shape may need to change too — the
deployment must fail loudly, not silently skip, if the platform contract changes
_deployment_history: depl_0132ARBCdsSRh6hxocbbW7ac (archived 2026-07-04): superseded when the reader-feedback feature added FEEDBACK_TOKEN_SECRET_ARN/FEEDBACK_BASE_URL to the initial_prompt's env exports -- deployments are immutable (PATCH/PUT return 405; only GET/HEAD/POST/DELETE are allowed on /v1/deployments/{id}), so an update means POST a new deployment (same agent/environment/schedule, revised initial_events) then POST .../archive on the old one (DELETE on an active deployment 404s; archiving is the actual mechanism despite DELETE appearing in the Allow header).

depl_018VbiHQAKzmnw2VDAZNtbe6 (archived 2026-07-04): superseded once DNS for feedback.mschweier.com was cut over -- flipped FEEDBACK_BASE_URL from the temporary CloudFront default domain to the real subdomain.

agent._comment: References the agent definition created via the Claude Console/API (docs/adr/0007). The skill (deploy/managed-agent/skills/daily-ai-brief/) is now a verified verbatim port of the real daily-ai-brief skill, incl. its real sources.md -- not a reconstruction. The skill deliberately does NOT touch Polly/SES/HTML/delivery (matches the real skill's own design: 'converting it to speech and delivering it is the caller's job'). This initial_prompt is that caller/wrapping layer -- it invokes the skill for the writing, then layers the delivery mechanics on top, mirroring how the local Desktop task's SKILL.md wraps the same skill today (its STEP 1 invokes the skill; STEPs 5-8 are the wrapping delivery task).
agent.initial_prompt: 0) Before researching: run `python3.13 /opt/pipeline/audio_email.py read-recent-briefs` (no arguments needed for the default; it does not require any of the send-mode env vars used in step 3 below). This reads the last few (default 3) most recent PRIOR briefs from the S3 briefs/ store (if any exist -- none on a first-ever run, or fewer than 3 early on, both fine) and writes each to its own /workspace/AI Brief - <its own actual date>.md, using whatever dates they actually are (which may span a weekend/holiday/missed run, not always the 3 most recent calendar days). 1) Invoke the daily-ai-brief skill to research, write, and validate today's brief and produce the listening script, per the skill's own output contract: the skill's own Step 1 will find the files from step 0 (if any) via its normal WORKING_FOLDER search and use them to avoid repeating stories across all of them and correctly label genuine follow-ups by each file's actual date, not by assuming any of them is from yesterday. It writes today's brief to /workspace/AI Brief - YYYY-MM-DD.md (today's date) and, since this is always the audio-requested case, save the listening script explicitly to /workspace/listening-script.txt (the skill defers this exact path to the caller). 2) As this deployment's own wrapping task, NOT the skill's job: convert that brief Markdown to clean, inbox-readable HTML and save to /workspace/brief.html. 3) Run the delivery step: `python3.13 /opt/pipeline/audio_email.py`, after exporting BRIEF_MARKDOWN_PATH (today's actual dated output file from step 1), BRIEF_HTML_PATH=/workspace/brief.html, LISTENING_SCRIPT_PATH=/workspace/listening-script.txt, MP3_OUT_PATH=/workspace/brief.mp3, EMAIL_SUBJECT='Daily AI Brief — <today, DD.MM.YYYY>', SUBSCRIBERS_TABLE_NAME=brief-subscribers, SUBSCRIBERS_API_BASE_URL=https://2il2bs0iq4.execute-api.us-east-1.amazonaws.com, FEEDBACK_TOKEN_SECRET_ARN=arn:aws:secretsmanager:us-east-1:740353583786:secret:daily-ai-brief/feedback-token-signing-secret-EsDW6s, FEEDBACK_BASE_URL=https://feedback.mschweier.com, PIPELINE_TIMEZONE=Europe/Berlin (matches this deployment's own schedule timezone -- must always match it, since this value determines both today's S3 archive date and how 'today' is resolved when step 0 looks for the most recent prior briefs). This synthesizes the narrated MP3 (async Polly, text-only fail-safe on any audio error), sends the owner's copy, fans out to confirmed subscribers (failure-isolated per recipient), embeds a personalized feedback link in every recipient's email when feedback config is present (never blocking the send if it's absent or fails), and archives the brief + HTML + script to S3 briefs/YYYY-MM-DD/ for tomorrow's step 0.
skills: daily-ai-brief (skill_01H2qu83NwnJ5zqcbrqsCcJ6, set on the agent itself -- not re-specified per-deployment)

environment._comment: The self_hosted environment id created via the Claude Console after the CDK stack (ManagedAgentSandboxStack) is deployed and its webhook is registered. See README step 2.

schedule._comment: Matches today's weekday 6:07 AM local run (docs/adr/0006). Confirmed with the owner 2026-07-03: actual local timezone is Europe/Berlin (the ADR's original America/Los_Angeles was an unconfirmed placeholder, corrected before deploy).

_manual_steps_not_encoded_here: Creating the agent (agent_id) and the self_hosted environment (environment_id) via the Claude Console/API.
Registering deploy/managed-agent's WebhookUrl stack output with Anthropic for session.status_run_started.
Generating and populating the environment key + webhook signing secret into the two Secrets Manager secrets this CDK stack creates empty.
Building and pushing the microVM container image (deploy/managed-agent/microvm/) via the reference implementation's build-image.sh-equivalent flow.
Actually calling the Deployments API (or Console) with this file's schedule/agent/environment values to create or update the live scheduled deployment.
See deploy/managed-agent/README.md for the full, ordered runbook.
